vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-636: Pi Coding Agent support as alternative harness to Claude Code

plan.title: Pi Coding Agent support as alternative harness to Claude Code
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: Panopticon today only supports Claude Code as an agent harness. That ties us to Anthropic's permission model, the fragile tmux paste-buffer IPC pattern, and Claude Code's bash-hook extensibility. Pi Coding Agent (badlogic/pi-mono) offers structured bidirectional RPC over stdin/stdout, a TypeScript extension system, native multi-provider support (Anthropic/OpenAI/Google/Mistral/Ollama/...), and inline cost reporting in its session JSONL. We need to integrate Pi as an additive second harness without disturbing Claude Code, while honoring Anthropic ToS rules around subscription auth.
narratives.Proposal: Add Pi as a second AgentRuntime adapter parallel to ClaudeCodeRuntime. Spawn Pi inside tmux with its stdin redirected from a per-agent mkfifo named pipe at ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/rpc.in so the dashboard delivers JSONL RPC commands without touching tmux paste-buffers. Vendor the lifecycle extension at packages/pi-extension/ and load it by absolute path (no npm publish). Parse Pi's tree-structured v3 JSONL via a new src/lib/cost-parsers/pi-parser.ts that walks the active branch and emits the existing SessionUsage shape so cost-monitor and dashboard cost views work unchanged. Expose harness+model pickers at the three user-initiated spawn surfaces (plan kickoff, work agent start, conversation panel) and per-specialist defaults in Settings. Gate Pi+Anthropic with a single canUseHarness(harness, model, authMode) helper that blocks the combination only when Anthropic auth=subscription. pan doctor checks for the Pi binary; pan sync writes ~/.pi/agent/settings.json so Pi loads skills from ~/.claude/skills. End-to-end smoke test verifies the full path: spawn → ready → prompt via fifo → tool execution → completion → cost parsed → resume.

items.title: Widen RuntimeName to 'claude-code' | 'pi' across types and contracts

narrative.Action: Update src/lib/runtimes/types.ts RuntimeName to 'claude-code' | 'pi'. Add a getHarness(snapshot) helper that normalizes AgentSnapshot.runtime to one of those values (defaulting unknown/legacy values to 'claude-code'). Audit packages/contracts/src/event-reducers.ts:431 where reducers default-fill 'claude' and migrate to 'claude-code' where the literal string is the wire value. Do NOT tighten AgentSnapshot.runtime to a Schema.Literal yet — keep optional string for forward compat, but route all reads through the helper.

subItems.title: RuntimeName type union is exactly 'claude-code' | 'pi' and the union is exported from src/lib/runtimes/types.ts
A getHarness(snapshot: AgentSnapshot): 'claude-code' | 'pi' helper exists, is unit-tested, and is the only place AgentSnapshot.runtime is read across src/dashboard/frontend/
packages/contracts/src/event-reducers.ts no longer writes the bare literal 'claude'; it writes 'claude-code' (or leaves the field unset when no harness is known)
npm run typecheck passes and no consumer of AgentSnapshot.runtime compares against the literal 'claude' anywhere in src/ or packages/

items.title: canUseHarness(harness, model, authMode) ToS gate helper

narrative.Action: Add a single function canUseHarness(harness: HarnessName, model: string, authMode: AuthMode) -> { allowed: boolean; reason?: string } in src/lib/cloister/router.ts (or a new src/lib/harness-policy.ts if cleaner). The only blocked cell: harness='pi' AND providerForModel(model)='anthropic' AND authMode='subscription'. Every spawn entry point AND every picker UI must call this. The reason string is human-readable and shown verbatim in the picker tooltip when an option is disabled.

subItems.title: canUseHarness exists and returns allowed=false with a non-empty reason for {harness:'pi', anthropic-model, authMode:'subscription'}
canUseHarness returns allowed=true for {harness:'pi', anthropic-model, authMode:'api-key'}
canUseHarness returns allowed=true for every {harness:'pi', non-anthropic-model, *} cell (openai/google/mistral/ollama/openrouter)
canUseHarness returns allowed=true for every {harness:'claude-code', *, *} cell
Vitest covers a 2 x 5 x 3 matrix (harness x provider x authMode) with explicit assertions per cell

items.title: pan doctor reports Pi binary status and supported version range

narrative.Action: Extend src/lib/prereqs/ (or wherever pan doctor checks live) with a Pi prereq. Run `pi --version`, parse the output, compare to a SUPPORTED_PI_VERSION_MIN constant pinned in code (start at 0.73.0 per PRD). Report: missing (with install hint), too-old, OK. Also verify packages/pi-extension/dist/index.js exists when Pi is detected — warn if missing and tell the user to run `npm run build` in that workspace.

subItems.title: pan doctor lists a 'Pi Coding Agent' check and reports OK/missing/too-old with the resolved version when Pi is on PATH
pan doctor warns when packages/pi-extension/dist/index.js does not exist and includes the build command in the warning
When Pi is missing, pan doctor exits non-zero only if --strict is passed; otherwise it warns and continues so existing Claude Code workflows are not blocked

items.title: Vendored @panopticon/pi-extension at packages/pi-extension/

narrative.Action: Create packages/pi-extension/ as a workspace package. TypeScript source under src/, tsdown build to dist/index.js. Entry exports default function(pi: ExtensionAPI) registering: pi.on('session_start') → write ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/ready.json and session.id; pi.on('tool_execution_end') → write ~/.panopticon/heartbeats/<agentId>.json with timestamp/tool/pid; pi.on('turn_end') → activity touch; pi.registerCommand('pan-done') → write ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/completed marker. agentId comes exclusively from process.env.PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID; never default. Add tsdown.config.ts. Add to root bun workspaces. No npm publish.

subItems.title: packages/pi-extension/dist/index.js builds via `bun install && cd packages/pi-extension && npm run build` and exports a default function
Vitest covers all four hooks: simulating each Pi event writes the expected file under a test-specific tmp HOME, and uses agentId from PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID
Two extension instances with different PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID values do not collide on heartbeat or ready files (covered by test)
Extension does NOT publish to npm (no `npm publish` in CI; package marked `private: true`)

items.title: Launcher generator emits Pi command lines (--mode rpc, --no-context-files, --extension)

narrative.Action: Extend src/lib/launcher-generator.ts: add a HarnessName field to LauncherConfig and a buildPiCommand path that emits `pi --mode rpc --model <model> --session-dir <agentDir>/sessions/ --extension <abs-path-to-pi-extension-dist> --no-context-files --append-system-prompt "$(cat <prompt-file>)"`. Stdin-from-fifo wiring goes here too: when config.harness === 'pi', the generator emits `exec pi ... < <fifoPath>` instead of the bare exec. Reuse the existing env/cd/permissionFlags scaffolding.

subItems.title: generateLauncherScript with harness='pi' includes `--mode rpc`, `--no-context-files`, and the absolute path to packages/pi-extension/dist/index.js as `--extension`
When config.harness === 'pi', the emitted command is `exec pi ... < <fifoPath>` (stdin redirected from the named pipe)
When config.harness === 'claude-code' (or unset), the existing claude/claudish output is bit-for-bit unchanged (covered by snapshot test)
Pi launcher does NOT emit --dangerously-skip-permissions or --permission-mode flags (Pi has no permission system)

items.title: Per-agent mkfifo named pipe creation and lifecycle

narrative.Action: Add helper functions in src/lib/runtimes/pi.ts (or src/lib/runtimes/pi-fifo.ts) to: create the agent dir if missing, mkfifo at ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/rpc.in (chmod 0600), and unlink it on agent kill. Use mkfifoSync from the `fs` module — node has no async mkfifo. The fifo writer side is opened lazily on first write. Adapter must wait for ready.json before opening write-side. NEVER call this from server route handlers; it's only invoked from spawnAgent and killAgent paths which run on dedicated handlers.

subItems.title: createPiFifo(agentId) creates ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/rpc.in with mode 0600 and is idempotent (unlinks existing fifo first)
destroyPiFifo(agentId) unlinks the fifo and is safe to call when the fifo does not exist

writePiCommand(agentId, json) opens the writer fd lazily, writes one JSONL line, and closes — covered by integration test that pre-creates a reader (cat fifo.in > out.log) and asserts the line appears
writePiCommand returns a typed error (PiNotReady) — and does NOT block — when ready.json is not present yet

items.title: src/lib/cost-parsers/pi-parser.ts: walk Pi tree JSONL active branch into SessionUsage

narrative.Action: Add src/lib/cost-parsers/pi-parser.ts. Read Pi v3 JSONL line by line. Build a Map<id, entry> and a parentId chain. Identify the latest leaf by max(timestamp) among entries with no children. Walk leaf→root via parentId, collecting message/usage/cost entries. Sum usage.input/output/cacheRead. Use entry.usage.cost.total directly as cost_v2 (Pi's inline cost). Map to existing SessionUsage interface from jsonl-parser.ts (do NOT redefine it). Handle compaction entries (subtract pre-compaction input that has been folded into a summary). Handle model_change entries (record model breakdown per segment). Emit a debug log with delta(piInlineCost - localRecomputedCost) for observability. Snapshot fixtures under src/lib/cost-parsers/__tests__/fixtures/pi/.

subItems.title: parsePiSession(filePath) returns a valid SessionUsage object for a linear (no-fork) Pi v3 JSONL file with correct totals
For a forked Pi session with two branches, parsePiSession returns totals for ONLY the active branch (latest leaf), not the union of all branches
For a session with one model_change entry, modelBreakdown contains both models with their respective tokens/cost
For a session with a compaction entry, input_tokens are NOT double-counted across the compaction boundary
Unknown event types are logged once per session and do not throw; parser returns whatever it could compute
cost_v2 equals the sum of entry.usage.cost.total along the active branch (Pi's inline cost is the canonical figure)

items.title: src/lib/runtimes/pi.ts implements AgentRuntime for Pi

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/runtimes/pi.ts exporting class PiRuntime implements AgentRuntime { name: 'pi' }. Methods: spawnAgent (creates fifo via pi-fifo-ipc, writes launcher.sh via pi-launcher-support, kicks off tmux session, waits up to 30s for ready.json before returning); sendMessage (writes {cmd:'prompt',text} to fifo via writePiCommand, errors with PiNotReady if no ready.json); killAgent (writes {cmd:'abort'} to fifo, waits 2s, then kill -TERM, then kill -KILL after 5s; unlinks fifo); getHeartbeat (reads ~/.panopticon/heartbeats/<agentId>.json, falls back to JSONL mtime + tmux pane activity); getTokenUsage / getSessionCost (delegate to parsePiSession on the agent's session JSONL under <agentDir>/sessions/); getSessionPath; isRunning; listSessions. Register PiRuntime in runtimes/index.ts.

subItems.title: PiRuntime is registered in getGlobalRegistry() alongside ClaudeCodeRuntime and getRuntime('pi') returns it

spawnAgent({harness:'pi',...}) creates the fifo, writes the launcher script, starts a tmux session, and resolves only after ready.json is detected (with a 30s timeout that throws a PiSpawnTimeout error)
sendMessage(agentId, text) writes a single {"cmd":"prompt","text":...} JSONL line to the fifo and resolves after the write completes; sendMessage before ready.json exists rejects with PiNotReady
getHeartbeat(agentId) returns a Heartbeat sourced from the heartbeat file when fresh (<60s old), falls back to JSONL mtime when stale, falls back to tmux activity when JSONL is missing
getSessionCost(agentId) returns a CostBreakdown derived from parsePiSession on the agent's active Pi session file

items.title: RuntimeRegistry.getRuntimeForAgent dispatches by agent state harness

narrative.Action: Replace the hardcoded `return this.get('claude-code')` in src/lib/runtimes/index.ts:60 with: read agent state, look up state.harness (default 'claude-code' if unset), and return this.get(state.harness). Audit every caller of getRuntimeForAgent and getRuntime('claude-code') and confirm none assumes claude-code-only behavior.

subItems.title: getRuntimeForAgent(agentId) reads state.harness and returns the matching registered runtime (claude-code or pi)
When state.harness is missing/legacy, getRuntimeForAgent returns the claude-code runtime (back-compat)
Vitest registers two runtime stubs and verifies dispatch on both branches
No remaining grep hit for `getRuntime('claude-code')` outside of the registry bootstrap and unit tests

items.title: Persist harness on agent state and route base command builder

narrative.Action: Extend AgentState in src/lib/agents.ts with a `harness: 'claude-code' | 'pi'` field. spawnAgent persists it from options.harness (default 'claude-code'). Refactor getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand(model) into getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand(model, harness) — when harness='pi', return the Pi command line; when harness='claude-code' (or unset), preserve existing claude/claudish behavior bit-for-bit. Update all four call sites: agents.ts:1100, agents.ts:1450, agents.ts:1808, planning/spawn-planning-session.ts:491 to pass the resolved harness.

subItems.title: AgentState carries a harness field; getAgentState/saveAgentState round-trip it through state.json
spawnAgent({harness:'pi',...}) records harness='pi' on state and the launcher uses Pi command lines
getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand has a (model, harness) signature; harness='claude-code' produces output identical to the pre-change implementation (snapshot test)
All four call sites pass an explicit harness; vitest covers harness='pi' producing a `pi --mode rpc ...` command

items.title: Extend cloister specialist_models config with per-role harness

narrative.Action: Extend ModelSelectionConfig in src/lib/cloister/config.ts with a parallel specialist_harnesses block: { merge_agent?: HarnessName; review_agent?: HarnessName; test_agent?: HarnessName; inspect_agent?: HarnessName; uat_agent?: HarnessName }. Defaults: 'claude-code' for every role. Wire load/save through loadCloisterConfig and the existing settings persistence. Do NOT couple harness and model into a struct — keep them as parallel maps so users can change one without re-confirming the other.

subItems.title: ModelSelectionConfig.specialist_harnesses exists with the same five role keys as specialist_models
Default config returns harness='claude-code' for every role when no user override is set
Settings persistence round-trips specialist_harnesses through the same yaml/json path as specialist_models (no schema migration needed for absent keys)

items.title: ModelRouter.getSpecialistHarness helper

narrative.Action: Add ModelRouter.getSpecialistHarness(specialistName: string): HarnessName mirroring getSpecialistModel. Reads modelSelection.specialist_harnesses and falls back to 'claude-code'.

subItems.title: getSpecialistHarness('review-agent') returns the configured harness for review_agent (normalizing dash to underscore)
Unknown specialist names fall back to 'claude-code'

items.title: Single resolveSpecialistBaseCommand(role, model, harness) and replace 3 hardcoded sites

narrative.Action: Add resolveSpecialistBaseCommand(role: string, model: string, harness: HarnessName) in src/lib/cloister/router.ts (or specialists.ts). For harness='claude-code', return the existing claude/claudish-derived command (delegates to the refactored getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand). For harness='pi', return a Pi command line equivalent. Replace the three hardcoded sites: src/lib/cloister/specialists.ts:1104, src/lib/cloister/specialists.ts:2316, src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts:577. The harness is resolved at call time via getSpecialistHarness, AND canUseHarness is consulted — if blocked, fall back to claude-code for that specialist and emit a warning log so a misconfigured Settings panel cannot crash a pipeline.

subItems.title: resolveSpecialistBaseCommand exists and is the only producer of specialist baseCommand strings (no remaining literal 'claude' baseCommand assignments in cloister/)
When canUseHarness returns allowed=false for the role's configured harness/model/auth combination, the helper falls back to harness='claude-code' and logs a warning with the reason
Vitest covers both harness branches AND the fallback path

items.title: Per-role harness dropdown in Settings page

narrative.Action: Locate the existing per-role model settings UI in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/ (search for specialist_models / role-based model selectors). Add a Harness dropdown next to each Model dropdown for the five specialist roles. Disabled options reflect canUseHarness with a hover tooltip showing the reason. Persist via the same settings save endpoint that handles specialist_models. Show a banner at the top of the Harness section if Anthropic auth is in subscription mode, explaining which combos are blocked.

subItems.title: Settings page shows a Harness dropdown for review_agent, test_agent, merge_agent, inspect_agent, uat_agent next to the existing Model dropdown
When Anthropic auth=subscription, selecting harness='pi' alongside an Anthropic model is visually disabled and a tooltip surfaces the canUseHarness reason text
Saving the form persists specialist_harnesses through the same API call that persists specialist_models (single submit, no separate endpoint)
Manual UI verification: open Settings in the running dashboard, change one role's harness, reload, verify persistence

items.title: Harness+model picker in plan kickoff UI

narrative.Action: In the dashboard's plan kickoff flow (find via grep for 'start planning' / 'plan-kickoff' in src/dashboard/frontend/), add a Harness selector alongside the existing Model selector. Selector enforces canUseHarness: invalid combos are disabled with reason tooltips. Selected harness is sent to the start-planning API and persisted as agent state harness. Default: claude-code (or whatever the user picked last for plan kickoff, stored in localStorage).

subItems.title: Plan kickoff UI shows a Harness dropdown next to the Model dropdown with claude-code and pi as options
Submit button is disabled (with the canUseHarness reason text) for blocked combinations
The selected harness is forwarded to the planning spawn endpoint and the resulting planning agent's state.harness reflects the selection

items.title: pan start --harness CLI flag and prompt fallback

narrative.Action: Add `--harness <claude-code|pi>` to `pan start <id>` (and `pan plan` if symmetric). When omitted, the CLI prompts (interactive) or falls back to the existing default for non-interactive contexts. canUseHarness is consulted before spawn — invalid combos exit non-zero with the reason text on stderr.

subItems.title: `pan start <id> --harness pi --model gpt-5.4` spawns a Pi work agent and persists harness on state
`pan start <id> --harness pi --model claude-opus-4-7` while Anthropic auth=subscription exits non-zero with the canUseHarness reason text
Without --harness, behavior matches pre-change (claude-code default; no breaking change for existing scripts)

items.title: Dashboard Start button harness+model selection

narrative.Action: In the dashboard's Start work agent UI, add a Harness selector alongside Model. Same canUseHarness gating as plan kickoff. Selection forwards to /api/agents/start (or whatever endpoint pan start hits server-side) and ends up on agent state.

subItems.title: Dashboard Start dialog shows a Harness dropdown that is functionally equivalent to the plan kickoff picker
End-to-end: starting a work agent with harness=pi from the dashboard results in a tmux session running `pi --mode rpc ...`

items.title: Conversation panel harness+model picker

narrative.Action: In the dashboard's free-form conversation panel (src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/conversations/ or similar), add a Harness selector alongside the existing Model selector when starting a new conversation. canUseHarness gating applies. Resume of an existing conversation reuses its persisted harness — no picker.

subItems.title: New conversation form shows a Harness dropdown alongside Model and respects canUseHarness
Resuming an existing conversation does NOT show the Harness picker and uses the conversation's persisted harness
End-to-end: a new conversation with harness=pi spawns a Pi process and the conversation's session JSONL is parsed by pi-parser

items.title: pan status and dashboard agent cards display harness

narrative.Action: Add a Harness column to `pan status` table output. Add a small badge/icon to dashboard AgentList / AgentDetailView cards indicating claude-code vs pi. Drives readability when running mixed-harness pipelines.

subItems.title: `pan status` output includes a Harness column with values 'claude-code' or 'pi'
Dashboard agent cards show a harness badge sourced from getHarness(snapshot)

items.title: pan sync writes ~/.pi/agent/settings.json so Pi reads skills from ~/.claude/skills

narrative.Action: In src/lib/sync.ts, when Pi is detected on PATH, ensure ~/.pi/agent/settings.json contains `"skills": ["~/.claude/skills"]`. Idempotent merge with any existing keys. Do NOT overwrite the file when Pi is not detected — that risks clobbering user config.

subItems.title: Running `pan sync` on a clean machine creates ~/.pi/agent/settings.json with skills containing ~/.claude/skills
Running `pan sync` when ~/.pi/agent/settings.json already has unrelated keys preserves them and only adds/updates the skills entry
Running `pan sync` when Pi is not on PATH does NOT touch ~/.pi/agent/settings.json

items.title: Resume a Pi agent session via stored session id

narrative.Action: After PiRuntime spawnAgent succeeds, the extension writes ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/session.id. On resume (e.g., dashboard restart, post-crash recovery), PiRuntime.spawnAgent reads session.id and adds `--session <id>` to the Pi command line. If the file is missing, it spawns a fresh session and logs a warning.

subItems.title: After a successful initial spawn, ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/session.id contains the Pi session id reported by the extension
Re-spawning the same agentId after a kill reuses the stored session id via `pi --session <id>`
When session.id is missing on resume, a fresh session is spawned and a warning is logged but the spawn does not fail

items.title: PiRuntime.killAgent: graceful RPC abort then SIGTERM then SIGKILL

narrative.Action: killAgent writes {cmd:'abort'} to the fifo, waits 2s for graceful shutdown (poll tmux pane PID exit), then SIGTERM the Pi process, waits 5s, then SIGKILL. Always unlinks the fifo and removes the per-agent dir's transient files (heartbeat, ready.json, completed marker). NEVER deletes session JSONL files (per CLAUDE.md sacred-jsonl rule).

subItems.title: killAgent on a healthy Pi process exits within 3s and the tmux session is gone
killAgent on a hung Pi process (ignoring abort) escalates to SIGTERM after 2s and SIGKILL after 7s total
killAgent unlinks rpc.in, ready.json, heartbeat, and completed marker but does NOT delete the session JSONL under <agentDir>/sessions/

items.title: End-to-end smoke test: spawn Pi agent, drive one prompt, assert lifecycle signals

narrative.Action: Add a vitest under src/lib/runtimes/__tests__/pi-e2e.test.ts (gated behind `if (process.env.PAN_E2E === '1')` so it doesn't run in standard CI without Pi installed). It: (1) spawns a Pi work agent for a fixture issue against a fake provider (or `pi --provider mock` if Pi supports it; otherwise targets ollama with a tiny local model), (2) waits for ready.json, (3) sends a prompt via fifo, (4) waits for /pan-done marker after a custom command, (5) parses the session JSONL via pi-parser and asserts non-zero token totals + non-zero cost_v2, (6) kills the agent and verifies cleanup. Documents how to run the test locally in docs/HARNESSES.md.

subItems.title: Test gated behind PAN_E2E=1 env var; CI default skips it
Test spawns a Pi agent, observes ready.json within 30s, sends a prompt that triggers tool execution, observes a heartbeat file refresh, observes /pan-done marker
Test verifies pi-parser returns non-zero tokens and non-zero cost_v2 from the resulting session JSONL
Test verifies killAgent cleans up rpc.in/ready.json/heartbeat but preserves the session JSONL

items.title: docs/HARNESSES.md — install Pi, harness selection, ToS rules

narrative.Action: Create docs/HARNESSES.md covering: (1) supported harnesses (Claude Code default, Pi alternative); (2) how to install Pi (npm i -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent or pi-mono installer); (3) where harness is selected (plan kickoff, work start, conversation panel, Settings per-specialist); (4) ToS rules (Pi+Anthropic blocked when Anthropic auth=subscription; subscription vs API key never both); (5) troubleshooting (pan doctor checks; how to run e2e smoke locally with PAN_E2E=1); (6) tradeoffs (RPC vs paste-buffer, multi-provider via Pi vs CLIProxy).

subItems.title: docs/HARNESSES.md exists and covers all six topics listed in the action
Top-level CLAUDE.md links to docs/HARNESSES.md from a new short 'Harnesses' section
